title: Antirhea urbaniana
taxonomy: Antirhea urbaniana belongs to the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, with over 13,000 species spread across 600 genera. The genus Antirhea itself is native to tropical regions of Central and South America, and is thought to be closely related to other genera within the Rubiaceae family. Antirhea urbaniana is a species of flowering plant that is still being studied and classified by botanists. Further research is needed to fully understand the classification and relationships of this plant within the Rubiaceae family.

intro: Antirhea urbaniana, also known as Urban's Antirhea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Antirhea urbaniana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, Antirhea urbaniana is often prepared as an infusion or decoction, and is also used in the form of essential oils, tinctures, and dried leaves, making it a popular ingredient in various herbal remedies and supplements.
